introSection: Ala Delta is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Quiqueland in Mexquitic de Carmona, San Luis Potosi, Mexico.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster opened in 2009.

Sometime before October 2012, the first drop was modified. It originally turned right before turning left toward the second chain lift, this was changed to have it immediately turn left.

By 2014, the coaster was left standing but not operating due to a blown lift motor.

Quiqueland closed its amusement park section in 2020, and as of 2024, Ala Delta is still standing.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Ala Delta is believed to be built in-house.

There is a climbing wall located on the side of the coaster's first lift hill.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The coaster's car is designed to look like a hang glider.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Video of Ala Delta on YouTube

fullText:
Ala Delta is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Quiqueland in Mexquitic de Carmona, San Luis Potosi, Mexico.

The coaster opened in 2009.

Sometime before October 2012, the first drop was modified. It originally turned right before turning left toward the second chain lift, this was changed to have it immediately turn left.

By 2014, the coaster was left standing but not operating due to a blown lift motor.

Quiqueland closed its amusement park section in 2020, and as of 2024, Ala Delta is still standing.

Ala Delta is believed to be built in-house.

There is a climbing wall located on the side of the coaster's first lift hill.

The coaster's car is designed to look like a hang glider.


 * Video of Ala Delta on YouTube
